title: 'w' flag drops previously set case-insensitivity
body: In Replacer::new (sd/src/replacer/mod.rs), when flags contains 'w' the code builds a brand new RegexBuilder for the word-boundary pattern, discarding a case_insensitive() call already applied earlier in the same flag string like 'iw'.

title: No short flag alias for the replacement count
body: sd only exposes the maximum-replacements-per-line option via a long flag in sd-cli/src/cli.rs; a short alias would match the ergonomics users expect from sed-like tools.

title: Binary input is processed the same as text
body: sd/src/input.rs reads stdin or files without detecting binary content first, so running sd against a binary file silently corrupts it instead of erroring out the way grep skips binary files by default.

title: Supported escape sequences aren't listed in --help
body: sd/src/unescape.rs recognizes sequences like \n, \t and \xNN in the replacement string, but sd-cli's help text doesn't enumerate which escapes are actually supported.
